calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-17-2008 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Nov-04-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove-Up Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Commissioner: William Gargano, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: L. Perez.
Douglas Holloway, Esq. for Plaintiff: Bank of the West.
Order waiving live testimony is in file.
After hearing the testimony and evidence presented, and considering of the declaration by Plaintiff's Witness Ernest Knowles, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendants: Rosin, Johnson, Gomez and Seca, Inc. dba Titan Engineering and Miguel Gomez as follows:
Principal $7,459.06
Prejudgment Interest $2,152.01
Attorney's Fees $2,500.00
Costs $532.25, for the Total of $12,643.32
Plaintiff's is also entitled to its immediate possession of the leasd quipment(s) described in the Jugment.
Judgment signed in open court.

Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And That Requests For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Bank of the West's motion to compel responses to interrogatories and for an order to deem certain matters admitted is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to provide complete verified responses without objections within 15 days of notice of this order. The matters set forth in the requests for admissions are ordered admitted. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $265 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
